revision.text:
[[ja:幣立神社]][[zh:幣立神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Heitate Shrine | native_name = 幣立神社 | image = File:Heitate Jingu.jpg | caption = Torii at Heitate Shrine | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|32.687056|N|131.135602|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 712 Ōno, Yamato, Kamimashiki District, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Kamurogi|ja|神漏岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamurogi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Kamurogi|ja|神漏美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamurogi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}
{{ill|Aso Twelve Deities|ja|阿蘇十二神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso Twelve Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿蘇神社|1=Aso Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q715639}} | established = Unknown | festivals = festival (September 15)
''Goshiki no Matsuri'' (August 23) | prefecture= {{ill|Yamato|qid=Q1184757|1=Yamato, Kumamoto}} }} '''Heitate Shrine''' (幣立神社, ''Heitate Jinja''), also known as '''Heitate Jingū''' (幣立神宮) or '''Hinomiyasan''' (日の宮), is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Yamato, Kumamoto|en|Yamato, Kumamoto|qid=Q1184757|lt=Yamato, Kumamoto|1=Yamato, Kumamoto}}, Japan. It holds the status of a former {{ill|district shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/district shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the pre-World War II Ranked Shinto shrines system. == History == The shrine's origins are steeped in legend. According to shrine tradition, {{ill|Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|ja|健磐龍命|1=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|4=fr|5=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|6=zh|7=健磐龍命|8=ko|9=다케이와타쓰노미코토|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}, a grandson of Emperor Jimmu, rested at this site during his journey to Mount Aso. He found the location to be ideal and established an altar to worship the {{ill|Tenjinchigi|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} (heavenly and earthly deities).{{Cite book|title=Aso Gunshi|year=1926|publisher=Kumamoto Education Society}} In the {{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|qid=Q1374325|lt=Engi Era|1=Engi (era)}} (901–923), Aso Daimyōji Tomonari built a shrine to honor the deities of the {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} and named it "Heitate-sha." In 1144, Aso Daimyōji Tomotaka incorporated the {{ill|Aso Twelve Deities|ja|阿蘇十二神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso Twelve Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿蘇神社|1=Aso Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q715639}} into the shrine as part of its pantheon, making it the regional tutelary shrine for Ōno-go.{{Cite book|title=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} The current shrine structures were renovated in 1729 by {{ill|Hosokawa Nobunori|ja|細川宣紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Nobunori|1=Hosokawa Nobunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605877}}, and in 1873, Heitate Shrine was designated a district shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system.{{Cite book|title=Shrines and Temples of Kumamoto|author=Takashi Suzuki|publisher=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun}} During the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|qid=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}} in 1904, a divine revelation included Heitate Shrine among eight shrines across Japan designated for victory prayers. The inclusion of this then-obscure shrine alongside prominent ''Ichinomiya'' shrines heightened its significance, inspiring local worshippers to plant commemorative trees known as the "Russo-Japanese War Sacred Trees." The shrine's proximity to {{ill|Takachiho|ja|高千穂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takachiho|1=takachiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11668869}} and its connections to various myths and legends have made it a point of interest for ancient history researchers and spiritual enthusiasts. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Kamurogi|ja|神漏岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamurogi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamurogi|ja|神漏美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamurogi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Aso Twelve Deities|ja|阿蘇十二神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso Twelve Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿蘇神社|1=Aso Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q715639}} == Sacred Trees == The shrine is known for its sacred trees: * '''Mansei Ikkei no Tenjinboku''': A sacred cypress tree near the main hall. * '''Gohyakue Sugi''': A 500-branch cedar along the approach. == festivals == * '''Goshiki no Matsuri''' (August 23): Honors the five directions and the harmony of the universe. * '''festival''' (September 15): Main celebration of the shrine. == Access == * '''Bus''': From {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|en|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|qid=Q6443418|lt=Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|1=Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal}}, take the Kumamoto Bus [M3-2] to Tsūjun Sansō, transfer to the Yamato Community Bus bound for Heitate Shrine. * '''Car''': From the {{ill|Kyushu Chūō Expressway|en|Kyushu Chūō Expressway|qid=Q17240197|lt=Kyushu Chuo Expressway|1=Kyushu Chūō Expressway}}, take the Yamato Tsūjunbashi Interchange and drive 16 km. == References == == External Links == * [https://kumamoto.guide/look/terakoya/104.html Official Tourism Information on Heitate Shrine] (in Japanese) {{Shinto-navbox}} {{translated page|ja|幣立神社|version=103528657|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:幣立神社]] [[zh:幣立神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11481888}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamato Town]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yamato, Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato, Kumamoto]]
